name: Chopard L.U.C XP Urushi ‘Crouching Tiger’
details: Chopard L.U.C XP Urushi 'Crouching Tiger' | Urushi is a traditional Japanese lacquering art, where the varnish is made from the sap of the Urushi tree (also known as the lacquer tree), which is mainly found in Japan and China. The resin is harvested once a year in small quantities, similar to how rubber is collected from the Hevea brasiliensis tree. These L.U.C XP Urushi timepieces were created in collaboration with Kiichiro Masumura, a world-renowned Urushi artist. The dials of the watches were hand-painted and embellished with gold dust by the Yamada Heiando Company, an official supplier to the Emperor of Japan. | Brand: Chopard | Model: L.U.C XP Urushi | Reference: 161902-5048 | Metal: Rose Gold | Box: Yes | Papers: Yes | Circa: 2022 | Dial: Hand Painted | Bracelet: Leather | Case Size: 39.5MM | Movement: Automatic | Condition: Excellent

name: Rolex Daytona 116520 Black APH Dial Stainless Steel 2014 Model
details: The Rolex Cosmograph Daytona reference 116520 in stainless steel, featuring the rare and highly sought-after APH dial variant. The APH dial refers to a well-documented typographic error in the word COSMOGRAPH printed on the dial, where the last three letters APH sit with an unusually wide gap between the R and the A. This kerning flaw was only produced in small numbers between roughly 2011 and 2014 before Rolex corrected it, making these examples particularly desirable to collectors in the know. Housed in the iconic 40mm Oyster case with tachymeter bezel, paired with the Oyster bracelet. It presents in great condition.This timepiece comes with its warranty card and original Rolex card wallet, as well as both hang tags. The bracelet has 11 links total. Please note the Daytona booklet is missing from the set.
